Tests the `arguments` object by verifying the length of arguments passed to a function.

# Purpose
This code defines a function `f_arg` that returns the `arguments` object, which contains all the arguments passed to the function. The `arguments` object is an array-like object accessible inside functions that contains the values of the arguments passed to that function. The code includes an assertion to verify that calling `f_arg` with three arguments results in an `arguments` object with a `length` of 3. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on demonstrating the use of the `arguments` object within a function.
